overview: An Alfred Hitchcock film from 1958. A retired detective trails a woman and is drawn into obsession.
title: Vertigo

overview: An Alfred Hitchcock film from 1954. A photographer with a broken leg suspects a neighbour of murder.
title: Rear Window

overview: An Alfred Hitchcock film from 1960. A secretary on the run checks into the Bates Motel.
title: Psycho

overview: An Alfred Hitchcock film from 1946. A woman is recruited to spy on a ring of Nazis in Brazil.
title: Notorious

overview: An Alfred Hitchcock film from 1959. An advertising man is mistaken for a spy and pursued across the country.
title: North by Northwest

overview: An Alfred Hitchcock film from 1963. A coastal town is besieged by inexplicable bird attacks.
title: The Birds

overview: A John Huston film from 1941. A private eye is hung up on the hunt for a priceless statuette.
title: The Maltese Falcon

overview: A Billy Wilder film from 1944. An insurance salesman is drawn into a murderous scheme by a married woman.
title: Double Indemnity

overview: A Billy Wilder film from 1950. A faded silent-era star clings to a young screenwriter in decaying Hollywood.
title: Sunset Boulevard

overview: An Orson Welles film from 1958. A corrupt police captain frames a man on the Mexican border.
title: Touch of Evil

overview: A Carol Reed film from 1949. A writer in postwar Vienna investigates the death of his friend Harry Lime.
title: The Third Man

overview: A John Huston film from 1950. A group of small-time crooks plan an elaborate jewellery heist.
title: The Asphalt Jungle

overview: A Howard Hawks film from 1946. A private detective is hired by a wealthy family and uncovers blackmail and murder.
title: The Big Sleep

overview: An Elia Kazan film from 1954. A dockworker stands up to mob corruption on the waterfront.
title: On the Waterfront

overview: A Roman Polanski film from 1974. A private eye in 1930s Los Angeles stumbles onto a water conspiracy.
title: Chinatown

overview: An Otto Preminger film from 1944. A detective falls for the portrait of a woman he believes is dead.
title: Laura

overview: A Michael Curtiz film from 1942. An American expatriate must choose between love and virtue in wartime Morocco.
title: Casablanca

overview: An Orson Welles film from 1941. A reporter investigates the dying word of a newspaper tycoon.
title: Citizen Kane
